tasks.title: Prime Number Identifier
tasks.description:
Conditional statements (if/else) allow your program to make decisions, executing different code paths based on whether a condition is true or false. Loops (like `for` or `while`) enable your program to repeat a block of code multiple times. Combining these, you can write programs that perform repetitive checks and make complex decisions, such as determining properties of numbers. A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.

Your program should read a single integer from standard input. It must then determine if this number is a prime number. If the number is prime, print "Prime". If it is not prime, print "Not Prime". Handle edge cases: numbers less than or equal to 1 are not considered prime. The input number will be between -100 and 1000.

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: 7 → The program checks for divisibility by numbers from 2 up to 3 (7/2). No divisors found, so the `is_prime` flag remains true. Output: Prime.
Input: 9 → The program checks for divisibility. When checking 3, 9 % 3 is 0, so the `is_prime` flag becomes false. Output: Not Prime.
